notes: Experimental thin-film polymer non-volatile memory; HP/Princeton 2003 discovery; parallel Intel/AMD/Thin Film Electronics efforts; never reached commercial volume; displaced by flash and later 3D NAND
JEDEC Double Data Rate 2 synchronous DRAM standard; 400-1066 MT/s; 1.8V; mainstream 2005-2008; replaced by DDR3 2007+
Non-volatile semiconductor memory; basis of USB drives, SSDs, SD cards, smartphone storage; displaced polymer memory approaches
Plastic optical discs; CD (1982), DVD (1995), Blu-ray (2006); largely displaced by flash storage and streaming by 2015
Persistent-memory technology announced 2015; shipped as Intel Optane DC; discontinued by Intel July 2022; Micron exited 2021
